code_scope.note: The actual validation is performed in the C library c-kzg-4844

summary: The c-kzg-4844 library performs array length validation before processing, preventing out-of-bounds access. Multiple validation layers provide defense-in-depth.

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Analyzed abstract state space for array inputs (commitments_bytes, cell_indices, cells, proofs_bytes). Identified potential state anomaly: mismatched array lengths could cause out-of-bounds access if not validated.

state_anomalies_found.anomaly: Length may differ from other input arrays
Length may differ from other input arrays
Length may differ from other input arrays
Length may differ from other input arrays

phase1_abstract_interpretation.potential_issue: If arrays have different lengths and this is not checked, array access using index from one array to access another could go out-of-bounds.

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Attempted to construct counterexample with mismatched array lengths. Found that c-kzg-4844 library validates array lengths at entry point.

counterexample.path_conditions: P2P peer sends verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch request with len(commitments) != len(cells)
counterexample.expected_outcome: Without validation: out-of-bounds read when accessing commitments[2]. With validation: early return with error.
counterexample.actual_behavior: c-kzg-4844 library checks that all input arrays have equal length in verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_rust() before any array access.

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: Counterexample is reachable from P2P network via DataColumnSidecar messages, but validation in c-kzg-4844 library prevents exploitation.
phase2_5_reachability_analysis.data_flow_path: P2P message → DataColumnSidecar deserialization → verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs() → verifyCellKzgProofBatch() → c-kzg-4844 library

validation_layers.effectiveness: Does NOT enforce array length equality; SSZ allows variable-length arrays to have different lengths
ENFORCES array length equality; returns error if arrays have mismatched lengths

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: The vulnerability would be exploitable if c-kzg-4844 did not validate array lengths. However, the library performs this check as the FIRST validation step, preventing out-of-bounds access. This is a defense-in-depth issue: Nimbus relies on c-kzg-4844's validation rather than validating in Nim code before calling the library.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Cannot prove invariant in Nimbus code, but can prove it holds in c-kzg-4844 library.
phase3_invariant_proving.proof_details: The c-kzg-4844 library enforces the invariant 'len(commitments) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs)' as a precondition check. The library returns an error if this condition is violated.

guard_identified.guard_condition: commitments.len() == cell_indices.len() && cell_indices.len() == cells.len() && cells.len() == proofs.len()
guard_identified.enforcement: Early return with C_KZG_BADARGS error code if arrays have mismatched lengths

phase3_invariant_proving.nimbus_layer_status: Nimbus does NOT validate array lengths before calling verifyCellKzgProofBatch(). It trusts the c-kzg-4844 library to perform this validation.
phase3_invariant_proving.defense_in_depth_recommendation: Add explicit length validation in verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs() before calling verifyCellKzgProofBatch() to fail fast and provide clearer error messages.

phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: Report to Nimbus developers as a robustness improvement. Add explicit array length validation in Nim code before calling c-kzg-4844 library.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: The vulnerability is NOT exploitable because c-kzg-4844 validates array lengths. However, relying solely on external library validation without defense-in-depth checks in application code is a maintenance risk. If c-kzg-4844 library were ever replaced or if a bug were introduced in the library, the vulnerability could become exploitable.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.severity_if_exploitable: HIGH (out-of-bounds memory access could crash >33% of nodes)
